title: Add --ignorefile flag to use alternate .dockerignore flags
body:
Allow users to override location of the .dockerignore file.
If user specified an --ignorefile flag, buildah will read the
file and pass in the exclude lines based on the .dockerignore
contents.

Also add a --contextdir flag to buildah copy and buildah add to
specify where the context directory is located.  For these two
commands the --ignorefile flag requires the --contextdir flag.

When the --ignorefile flag is passed in, the .dockerignore files
in the context directory will be ignored.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @nalind I need some help debugging this.  I must be doing something wrong because the excludes never seem to fire unless I exclude everything on buildah copy?

Oh right, a .dockerignore file is always evaluated relative to the context directory.  As a starting point, I'm guessing you'll want to set that to the directory that contains the file.  That will also cause us to chroot() into the directory, so symlinks that point out of that directory will be interpreted differently.  Either that, or the logic we're adding will need to prepend some path to the patterns that it's reading, taking care to correctly handle exclusions, which are patterns that begin with `!`.
